title: Week 10 - Sorting Algorithms I
description: Practice comparison, swapping, selection sort, bubble sort, and swap-count statistics.

questions.title: Swap Two Adjacent Height Values That Are Out of Order

description.background: A line of students' heights is stored in the array heights. The teacher notices that the student at position index and the student at position index + 1 may be out of order. If the first is taller than the second, they need to be swapped.
description.task: Check two adjacent heights and swap them if their order is wrong.
description.input: One array heights and one number index.
description.hint: Only two adjacent positions need to be compared.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(heights, index)
    if heights[index] > heights[index + 1] then
        local temp = heights[index]
        heights[index] = heights[index + 1]
        heights[index + 1] = temp
    end

    return heights
end

questions.title: Find the Position of the Minimum Value in a Score List

description.background: The first step of selection sort usually needs to find the position of the minimum value in a group of data.
description.task: Find the position of the minimum score in the scores array.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the position of the minimum value.
description.hint: You can first assume the first position is the minimum, then compare each item.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local minIndex = 1

    for i = 2, n do
        if scores[i] < scores[minIndex] then
            minIndex = i
        end
    end

    return minIndex
end

questions.title: Complete the First Round of Selection Sort

description.background: Each round of selection sort finds the smallest value in the remaining data and puts it at the current front position.
description.task: Complete the first round of selection sort by swapping the minimum value into position 1.
description.input: One array numbers and one number n.
description.output: Return the numbers array after the first round.
description.hint: Find the minimum position first, then swap it with the first position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(numbers, n)
    local minIndex = 1

    for i = 2, n do
        if numbers[i] < numbers[minIndex] then
            minIndex = i
        end
    end

    local temp = numbers[1]
    numbers[1] = numbers[minIndex]
    numbers[minIndex] = temp

    return numbers
end

questions.title: Use Selection Sort to Organize a Game Score Ranking

description.background: Game scores are stored in the array scores. The system needs to arrange the scores from low to high so it is easy to view the ranking from bottom to top.
description.task: Use selection sort to sort scores from small to large.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.hint: In each round, put the smallest value from the remaining data at the current front position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local minIndex = i

        for j = i + 1, n do
            if scores[j] < scores[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end

        local temp = scores[i]
        scores[i] = scores[minIndex]
        scores[minIndex] = temp
    end

    return scores
end

questions.title: Use Selection Sort to Sort Book Numbers from Small to Large

description.background: The library receives a batch of new books, and the book numbers are out of order. The administrator needs to arrange the book numbers from small to large.
description.task: Use selection sort to organize the books array.
description.input: One array books and one number n.
description.output: Return the books array sorted from small to large.
description.hint: The sorting process needs to find the smallest number in the current range many times.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(books, n)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local minIndex = i

        for j = i + 1, n do
            if books[j] < books[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end

        local temp = books[i]
        books[i] = books[minIndex]
        books[minIndex] = temp
    end

    return books
end

questions.title: Complete One Left-to-Right Pass of Bubble Sort

description.background: Bubble sort repeatedly compares adjacent data and swaps them if their order is wrong. After one pass, larger data moves toward the back.
description.task: Complete one left-to-right bubble pass on numbers and count the swaps in this pass.
description.input: One array numbers and one number n.
description.output: Return the processed numbers array and the swap count in order.
description.hint: Do only one pass of adjacent comparisons; full sorting is not needed.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(numbers, n)
    local swaps = 0

    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        if numbers[i] > numbers[i + 1] then
            local temp = numbers[i]
            numbers[i] = numbers[i + 1]
            numbers[i + 1] = temp
            swaps = swaps + 1
        end
    end

    return numbers, swaps
end

questions.title: Use Bubble Sort to Arrange Scores from High to Low

description.background: Competition scores are stored in the array scores. The leaderboard needs to show scores from high to low.
description.task: Use bubble sort to sort scores from large to small.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the scores array sorted from high to low.
description.hint: When comparing adjacent values, the larger score should move toward the front.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        for j = 1, n - i do
            if scores[j] < scores[j + 1] then
                local temp = scores[j]
                scores[j] = scores[j + 1]
                scores[j + 1] = temp
            end
        end
    end

    return scores
end

questions.title: Count Swaps When Bubble Sorting a Seat Table

description.background: Seat numbers are stored in the array seats. The teacher wants to use bubble sort to arrange the numbers from small to large and also wants to know how many seat swaps happen.
description.task: Use bubble sort to organize seats and count the total number of swaps.
description.input: One array seats and one number n.
description.output: Return the sorted seats array and the swap count in order.
description.hint: Increase the swap count only when an adjacent swap actually happens.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(seats, n)
    local swaps = 0

    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        for j = 1, n - i do
            if seats[j] > seats[j + 1] then
                local temp = seats[j]
                seats[j] = seats[j + 1]
                seats[j + 1] = temp
                swaps = swaps + 1
            end
        end
    end

    return seats, swaps
end

questions.title: Compare Selection Sort and Bubble Sort Swap Counts on the Same Data

description.background: The same data can be organized with selection sort or bubble sort. The teacher wants to compare how many swaps the two sorting methods make on the same data.
description.task: Copy the original array, sort one copy with selection sort and one copy with bubble sort from small to large, then return the two swap counts.
description.input: One array numbers and one number n.
description.output: Return the selection-sort swap count and bubble-sort swap count in order.
description.hint: The two sorts need to operate on their own arrays so they do not affect each other.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(numbers, n)
    local a = {}
    local b = {}

    for i = 1, n do
        a[i] = numbers[i]
        b[i] = numbers[i]
    end

    local selectionSwaps = 0
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local minIndex = i
        for j = i + 1, n do
            if a[j] < a[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end

        if minIndex ~= i then
            local temp = a[i]
            a[i] = a[minIndex]
            a[minIndex] = temp
            selectionSwaps = selectionSwaps + 1
        end
    end

    local bubbleSwaps = 0
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        for j = 1, n - i do
            if b[j] > b[j + 1] then
                local temp = b[j]
                b[j] = b[j + 1]
                b[j + 1] = temp
                bubbleSwaps = bubbleSwaps + 1
            end
        end
    end

    return selectionSwaps, bubbleSwaps
end
